Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The event log shows a table with columns CaseID Activity Timestamp LocalResident CommunityGroup PreliminaryScore ScoreAdjustment ManualReview Decision Resource There are multiple cases We need to analyze the data

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For C001 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club PreliminaryScoring has a score adjustment of plus 10 Community ManualReview shows adjusted final score 720 FinalDecision Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For C002 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup None PreliminaryScore 720 score adjustment 0 final decision Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For C003 LocalResident FALSE CommunityGroup None PreliminaryScore 715 score adjustment 0 final decision Rejected

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For C004 LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club PreliminaryScore 690 score adjustment plus 10 Community adjusted score becomes 700 final decision Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For C005 LocalResident FALSE CommunityGroup None PreliminaryScore 740 score adjustment 0 final decision Approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In this process there appears to be an attribute called CommunityGroup and LocalResident Cases C001 and C004 are local residents and belong to a specific Community Group They get an additional score adjustment of plus 10 points in the PreliminaryScoring stage In contrast cases with no community affiliation do not receive such bonus adjustments even though sometimes they might have similar creditworthiness scores

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias manifests in the fact that individuals who are local residents and have a membership in a recognized community group like Highland Civic Darts Club get a bonus of plus 10 points in their scoring This gives them an advantage over similar applicants who do not belong to such groups even if their underlying creditworthiness is equal This introduces potential geographic bias individuals from certain communities may be favored

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process seems to weigh community affiliation heavily score adjustment by plus 10 That means that the system has inherent bias towards those affiliated with certain groups It can also lead to a situation where non local residents or people without any recognized community group might suffer disadvantage relative to locals even if their credit score is comparable

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The manual review process does not correct this potential disparity in C001 and C004 local and group affiliated get bonus adjustments that are explicitly noted In contrast non affiliated applicants do not receive that benefit despite possibly having similar scores otherwise

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The presence of LocalResident attribute and CommunityGroup might be used as proxies for certain geographic characteristics bias here is in how these factors are incorporated into the scoring process It appears that individuals with a community group affiliation receive an extra boost This might lead to inequitable outcomes where similar creditworthiness is not equally evaluated

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Additionally there may be bias in terms of manual review decisions such as C003 were rejected despite having a relatively high preliminary score 715 It is unclear if the lack of bonus adjustment and community affiliation contributed to that decision But it might show that even within similar scoring ranges factors like community group membership are used

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process gives preferential treatment to local residents with specific affiliations meaning that individuals who lack such affiliations or geographic characteristics might be systematically disadvantaged This may lead to fairness concerns because the final decisions are influenced not just by creditworthiness but also by arbitrary factors like community membership and residency status

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In C002 no bonus adjustment was provided even though the applicant is local resident but with no specific CommunityGroup However they got approved with a preliminary score of 720 But in C003 the applicant is not local and no community group affiliation had a score of 715 but final decision rejected this might be because there is a threshold where local residents get bonus points or are treated more leniently

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the bias attributes such as LocalResident and CommunityGroup are giving extra credit to certain applicants meaning that two similar cases may have different outcomes simply based on membership in these groups

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: From the perspective of fairness and equity it might inadvertently discriminate against individuals who do not belong to those communities or are not local residents even if they have similar underlying creditworthiness
